page: Microsoft Azure D64_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 64 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 32 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8370C processor running at 2.8 GHz. The architecture is x86_64, supporting hyper-threading with 2.0 threads per physical core. The CPU includes 48 KB L1d cache, 1 MB L2 cache, and 48 MB L3 cache. It supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512, including AVX512F and AVX512_VNNI, alongside AES cryptography and SHA extensions. The instance is equipped with 256.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core capabilities and memory throughput. The instance achieves a strong multi-core CPU performance score of 62145.09 in stress-ng and a Geekbench multi-core score of 16499.0. Memory bandwidth is also strong, reaching 294.62 GB/sec for large block read-only operations and 135.63 GB/sec for large block write operations. Single-core performance is average, with a stress-ng single-core score of 1899.49 and a Geekbench single-core score of 1665.0. Cryptographic operations and database tasks perform in the top tier, as evidenced by a Passmark CPU encryption score of 58291.32 and Passmark database operations score of 29662.07. Redis SET operations reach 62.27 million operations per second.

While the instance demonstrates strong throughput for multi-threaded tasks, software compilation, and static web serving, it exhibits limitations in large-scale machine learning tasks. LLM inference speed for text generation using a large 70B model is weak, yielding only 2.61 tokens per second, though prompt processing for the same model remains average at 12.19 tokens per second. Given these characteristics, the D64_v5 is suited for enterprise application servers, multi-threaded database operations, static web serving, and software compilation pipelines. The lack of local storage requires external block storage attachment for persistent data.
description: The Microsoft Azure D64_v5 is an x86_64 general-purpose virtual machine featuring 64 dedicated vCPUs and 256.0 GB of RAM, running on an Intel Xeon 8370C processor at 2.8 GHz. Utilizing the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance provides a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per vCPU but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Benchmark results show strong multi-core performance, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 62145.09 and a Passmark CPU score of 60521.26. Large-block memory bandwidth is also strong, supporting high-throughput database operations and static web serving. However, single-core performance is average, and text generation for large 70B LLM models is weak at 2.61 tokens per second. This instance is suited for multi-threaded enterprise workloads, database operations, and software compilation, where high core density and balanced memory allocation are required.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D64_v5 is an x86_64 general-purpose instance with 64 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon 8370C processor, delivering strong multi-core and database performance without local storage.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D64_v5 is a general-purpose instance featuring 64 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, optimized for multi-core workloads.
tagline: A high-core general-purpose instance featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and balanced memory allocation for demanding multi-threaded enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor at 2.8 GHz
256.0 GB of system memory providing a balanced 4.0 GB per vCPU
Strong multi-core performance and large-block memory bandwidth
No local storage or GPU hardware included
Suited for multi-threaded databases, web serving, and software compilation
